infeasible_scenarios.scenarios: Live short-term cache vs. Message Persistence behavior under real disconnect/reconnect
infeasible_scenarios.reasoning: Distinguishing the ~2–20 minute short-term cache from the persistent History API in a real reconnect flow requires a live PubNub keyset and an actual network drop. The eval can verify code patterns (PNReconnectedCategory handling, restore: false) but cannot exercise the timing-dependent server-side behavior.

infeasible_scenarios.scenarios: End-to-end retention boundary verification (e.g., messages expiring after the configured period)
infeasible_scenarios.reasoning: Verifying that a message is no longer retrievable after the retention window has elapsed requires waiting for real-world time to pass (hours to days) against a live keyset. This cannot be reproduced in a sandboxed file-based eval.

Confirming that the Delete-from-History add-on is enabled and that the secret key is correctly scoped requires API access to a real PubNub Admin Portal account. The eval can verify that the agent calls pubnub.deleteMessages with the correct shape and notes server-only usage, but cannot prove the call would succeed against a real keyset.
